task_completion.description: Open the Viagra dataset in JASP.
task_completion.visual_evidence: JASP application is open and displays the Viagra dataset, with columns like 'Dose' and 'Libido' visible.

task_completion.description: Select and start the Bain ANOVA analysis.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Bain ANOVA analysis panel is visible in the JASP interface.

task_completion.description: Specify the hypothesis constraint that Libido increases with Dose.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Bain analysis options, a constraint like '1<2<3', 'dose1<dose2<dose3', or 'dose 1 < dose 2 < dose 3' is entered in the hypothesis specification box.

task_completion.description: Save the JASP project as Viagra_Bain.jasp.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The JASP window title shows 'Viagra_Bain.jasp' or a file manager/terminal shows the file saved at /home/ga/Documents/JASP/Viagra_Bain.jasp.

task_completion.description: Record the resulting Posterior Probability in bain_results.txt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal shows the file 'bain_results.txt' containing a posterior probability value (P(M|data)) that corresponds to the JASP output.

integrity.description: The agent used the JASP GUI to perform the analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the JASP GUI being actively used (e.g., clicking menus, typing in fields), rather than just terminal commands or scripts.

integrity.description: The recorded probability matches the actual JASP output.
integrity.visual_evidence: The posterior probability recorded in the text file matches the value displayed in the JASP results pane. It should not be blindly set to 0.92 if JASP shows a different value (e.g., ~0.801 or ~0.728).

integrity.description: The agent did not use a script to bypass JASP.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of a python or bash script generating the .jasp file directly without opening the JASP application.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected JASP project file is /home/ga/Documents/JASP/Viagra_Bain.jasp. The expected text file is /home/ga/Documents/JASP/bain_results.txt. The dataset is located at /home/ga/Documents/JASP/Viagra.csv. The required analysis type is BainAnova. The constraint signature should be one of: '1<2<3', 'dose1<dose2<dose3', or 'dose 1 < dose 2 < dose 3'. Note: The actual posterior probability produced by JASP may be around 0.801 or 0.728, despite the original task metadata expecting 0.92. Accept the probability that matches the actual JASP output screen.
